← the Atlas

Live screencast clips on the welcome page

feature · seedling ·proposed ·JUN 06, 2026

Embed short, crisp, looping .mp4/.webm clips of a real running kolu in kolu.dev/welcome — captured by the existing Cucumber + Playwright harness, transcoded for the web, regenerable by a just recipe. Plan of record.

Contents

  • The pipeline — reuse evidence capture, fork at transcode
  • Crisp by construction
  • The embed — a poster-first looping video
  • Reproducible, not hand-recorded
  • Prototype — the thinnest end-to-end slice
    • Pitfalls
    • Decisions
  • Architecture — capture engine vs. recordings (the electricity call)
  • Log — build journal

The kolu.dev/welcome guide tells you what kolu does in prose and static terminal mockups. This note adds the thing prose can’t carry: short, looping clips of a real kolu actually doing the thing — open a repo, launch an agent, watch the dock pulse — embedded inline next to the section that describes it, and (later) promoted to the homepage hero.

The capture mechanism already exists — it’s the

video-evidence

substrate (KOLU_EVIDENCE=1 → Playwright recordVideo on the Cucumber harness). The work here is not a new recorder; it’s reusing that capture and branching at transcode for a different audience. PR evidence optimizes for proof and degrades to a 12 fps GIF; a marketing clip optimizes for crispness and must never touch GIF. One load-bearing constraint drives every decision below: the clip has to look sharp — no low-quality nonsense.

The pipeline — reuse evidence capture, fork at transcode

Capture is a solved problem in this repo. packages/tests/support/hooks.ts already gates recordVideo on KOLU_EVIDENCE and pins recordVideo.size to the evidence viewport, and the ~880-line step library drives every interaction. A marketing clip reuses all of it: author (or pick) a scenario, select it by --name, get a video out. No parallel harness, no hand-driven clicks.

What changes is everything downstream of the raw capture:

PR evidence (existing)Marketing clip (this note)
Audiencea reviewer, onceevery visitor, repeatedly
Final forminline GIF + Pages player<video> loop, mp4 + webm
Priority”proves it works”crisp + small + autoplay
Hostingevidence-assets releasecommitted under website/public/
Lifetimethrowawayregenerated when the UI moves

So the two pipelines share the capture stage and diverge at transcode. That keeps the new surface area tiny — a transcode recipe and an embed — and means a single seeded scenario can feed both a PR-evidence GIF and a welcome clip.

Crisp by construction

This is the section the “no low-quality nonsense” rule lives in. There are two quality ceilings, and only one is fixable in post.

The transcode is the easy half. Never ship GIF (H.264 is 10–50× smaller for the same frames). Emit two web assets from the source, audio-free:

# Universal, crisp, fast-start. crf 18 ≈ visually lossless for screen content.
ffmpeg -i demo.src.mkv \
  -c:v libx264 -pix_fmt yuv420p -crf 18 -preset slow \
  -vf "scale=trunc(iw/2)*2:trunc(ih/2)*2" -an -movflags +faststart demo.mp4

# Smaller modern path, served first. VP9 CRF ~31 ≈ x264 crf 18 — NOT 18.
ffmpeg -i demo.src.mkv -c:v libvpx-vp9 -b:v 0 -crf 31 -row-mt 1 -an demo.webm

# Exact first frame as the poster (identical to frame 1 → no LCP flash).
ffmpeg -i demo.src.mkv -frames:v 1 -update 1 -q:v 2 poster.webp

The real ceiling: Playwright's recordVideo is hardcoded VP8 @ ~1 Mbit/s

Playwright’s recordVideo encodes VP8 at a fixed ~1 Mbit/s, 25 fps, no quality knob — the flags don’t exist (videos docs, issues #17217 / #31424). At 720p of dense xterm.js text, 1 Mbit/s VP8 shimmers — and no ffmpeg pass can recover detail the VP8 stage already threw away. A crf 18 mp4 transcoded from a 1 Mbit/s webm just faithfully preserves blur. So for a marketing-grade clip the source must be higher fidelity than recordVideo can produce.

Two levers make it genuinely crisp:

  • Capture above display size. The clip renders at maybe ~720–900px wide on the page; capture at 2× device scale factor (deviceScaleFactor: 2) and crop to the relevant region (one tile + dock, not a full 1280×720 desktop). Downscaling a 2560-wide source to an 800px element is what “retina” looks like. Keep recordVideo.size == viewport regardless — the default silently downscales to fit 800×800 and blurs text (pitfall #1).
  • For the shipped asset, capture lossless frames, not VP8. Swap recordVideo for CDP per-frame capture (Page.captureScreenshot PNG per tick, or page.screencast on Playwright ≥1.59 / the chrome-devtools MCP screencast_start) → assemble with ffmpeg from PNGs → crf 18. A lossless source removes the only ceiling we can’t transcode around. recommended for ship

Decision: prototype on recordVideo, ship on frame-capture

Use the existing recordVideo path for the prototype — zero new plumbing, proves the scenario and embed end-to-end. Judge the result at 100% on a retina display. If xterm text shimmers (it likely will at 720p VP8), promote that one clip to the frame-capture source. Don’t pre-build frame-capture before seeing the VP8 fail — but don’t ship VP8 to a marketing page if it does.

The embed — a poster-first looping video

The asset lives at website/public/demo/<clip>.{mp4,webm} + poster.webp and is referenced from website/src/pages/welcome.astro with the pattern every serious dev-tool site (Zed, Linear, Warp) converges on:

<video autoplay muted loop playsinline preload="metadata" poster="/demo/first-agent.webp">
  <source src="/demo/first-agent.webm" type="video/webm" />  <!-- VP9 first: smaller -->
  <source src="/demo/first-agent.mp4"  type="video/mp4"  />  <!-- H.264 fallback -->
</video>

Non-negotiables, each a real failure mode if dropped:

  • muted — browsers block autoplay for anything with sound; muted is the exemption (MDN Autoplay).
  • playsinline — without it, iOS Safari forces the clip fullscreen on play (WebKit).
  • WebM <source> first — the browser picks the first decodable source top-to-bottom; mp4-first makes Chrome/Firefox fetch the bigger file.
  • Poster = frame 1 — exported above, so first paint is instant and the swap to video is seamless (no flash). Below the fold, switch to preload="none" + an IntersectionObserver.

Reduced motion is mandatory, not a nicety

An autoplaying loop is motion. Gate it on prefers-reduced-motion: reduce: when set, render the poster only (no autoplay) and a visible play control. The welcome page is plain Astro HTML — a tiny inline <script> reading matchMedia('(prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)') to strip autoplay is enough (no SolidJS / @solid-primitives/media needed here — that primitive is for the in-app client, not the static site). Also call .play().catch() so iOS low-power-mode rejection falls back to the poster gracefully.

Reproducible, not hand-recorded

A clip recorded by hand goes stale silently and can’t be refreshed when the UI moves. Make it a build artifact. The capture rides a seeded scenario; five levers keep it byte-stable enough to regenerate:

  1. A seeded demo scenario reusing the step library (deterministic worktree names, fake-agent output, repo fixtures — the harness already scaffolds these).
  2. Freeze time — Playwright Clock.setFixedTime, installed before page.goto() (clock docs).
  3. Motion policy is inverted vs. evidence. hooks.ts injects prefers-reduced-motion: reduce for test determinism — a marketing clip is about the motion (the dock pulse), so the demo run must skip that animations-off init (same carve-out the video-evidence note flags), while still pacing with wait-for-condition, not sleep.
  4. Seed randomness — pin Math.random/UUID sources; crypto.randomUUID() is a top cause of run-to-run drift.
  5. Pin viewport / DPR / fonts and run in the harness’s standard environment so rendering matches.

Wrap it as a just recipe pair mirroring the house pattern — just website::demo-preview (capture + transcode to a scratch dir for eyeballing) and just website::demo-update (regenerate + stage website/public/demo/).

Decision: commit the clips, don't release-host them

PR evidence lives on the evidence-assets release because GitHub won’t embed a committed mp4 in a comment. The website has no such constraint — Astro serves public/ directly, and the clips are part of the page. Commit them (a few small mp4/webm under website/public/demo/), so the site builds reproducibly with no external fetch. Keep each clip in the single-MB range; if a clip ever blows past that, revisit hosting then.

Prototype — the thinnest end-to-end slice

One clip, the “First 5 minutes” section: open a repo → launch an agent → the dock pulses. Smallest path that proves capture, transcode, and embed together.

# packages/tests/features/welcome-demo.feature  (reuses existing steps)
@demo
Scenario: Demo — open a repo and launch an agent
  Given the terminal is ready
  When I create a terminal in the "kolu" repo running "claude"
  Then the dock row for that terminal pulses
  # a trailing dwell step holds the final frame for the loop seam
# 1. capture (prototype: reuse the evidence recordVideo path)
KOLU_EVIDENCE=1 just test-quick features/welcome-demo.feature --name "Demo — open a repo and launch an agent"

# 2. transcode the .webm → web assets (see "Crisp by construction")
ffmpeg -i reports/videos/<scenario>.webm -c:v libx264 -pix_fmt yuv420p -crf 18 \
  -preset slow -vf "scale=trunc(iw/2)*2:trunc(ih/2)*2" -an -movflags +faststart \
  website/public/demo/first-agent.mp4
ffmpeg -i reports/videos/<scenario>.webm -c:v libvpx-vp9 -b:v 0 -crf 31 -row-mt 1 -an \
  website/public/demo/first-agent.webm
ffmpeg -i website/public/demo/first-agent.mp4 -frames:v 1 -update 1 -q:v 2 \
  website/public/demo/first-agent.webp

# 3. embed the <video> in the "First 5 minutes" section of welcome.astro, build
just website::build

Judge frame 2’s text at 100% on retina. Crisp → wire the just recipe and add a second clip. Shimmering → promote to the frame-capture source before shipping.

  • ▸ Prototype clipSeeded welcome-demo.feature, recordVideo capture, transcode by hand, embed one poster-first <video> in “First 5 minutes”. Eyeball crispness.
  • ○ Crisp source if neededIf VP8/720p shimmers, swap the clip’s capture to CDP per-frame PNG → ffmpeg crf 18.
  • ○ just recipewebsite::demo-preview / website::demo-update — regenerate + stage public/demo/; reduced-motion gating in the embed.
  • ○ More clips + homepage heroA clip per guide section; promote the strongest to the index.astro hero.

Pitfalls

The traps, condensed

  • Transcoding VP8 and calling it crisp. 1 Mbit/s VP8 is the ceiling; ffmpeg can’t recover it. Capture lossless if it shimmers.
  • Default recordVideo size. Pin it to the viewport or text blurs from the 800×800 downscale.
  • VP9 CRF copied from x264. Use ~31, not ~18, or the webm bloats past the mp4.
  • Missing muted / playsinline. No autoplay anywhere / forced fullscreen on iOS.
  • mp4-first <source>. Wastes bytes — webm first.
  • Animations-off bleed-through. The evidence harness suppresses motion; the marketing run must skip that init or you film a still.
  • Real-time frame drift. recordVideo/screencast capture in real time, so smoothness varies run-to-run; only reach for beginFrame/virtual-time capture if a clip must be frame-perfect.

Decisions

  • Reuse the Cucumber harness for capture — same scenario can feed PR evidence and the welcome clip; no second recorder.
  • Fork at transcode, never GIF — H.264 mp4 + VP9 webm + WebP poster.
  • Prototype on recordVideo, ship on frame-capture if VP8 shimmers — don’t pre-build the crisp path; don’t ship the blurry one.
  • Commit clips under website/public/demo/ — not the evidence release; the site needs them at build time, single-MB each.
  • Static-site embed, not the SolidJS client — a plain inline <script> handles reduced-motion; no @solid-primitives dependency on the marketing page.

Architecture — capture engine vs. recordings (the electricity call)

Read through electricity.mdx, this work falls on two sides of the domain line, and conflating them is the trap to avoid.

  • The capture engine is a graduation candidate, not yet electricity. ① agnostic — “drive a headful browser at 2× under Xvfb, x11grab the framebuffer, transcode to web assets” names no terminal/canvas/git; it would screencast any web app. ② hard volatility — Xvfb lifecycle, app-mode launch, the fps-vs-resolution wall, x11grab/ffmpeg flags, SIGINT finalize, the ffmpeg-full nix gotcha — not a 3-line helper. ③ graduates — aspirationally only: drishti (surface’s real 2nd consumer) or any project could make its own clips, but no second consumer exists yet. Verdict, per the note’s own traps: isolate it into its own folder with the dependency arrow pointing out (nix deps + .ts runner), flagged publish? like createSharedRoot — mint @kolu/web-screencast and claim electricity only when a second consumer is real. Do not bury it in the app (trap #2: location is structure); do not call it electricity on agnosticism alone (trap #3).

  • The recordings are kolu domain. Each recording’s script (new terminal → git clone → claude) and its display tweaks (hide the right panel, collapse the dock — enacted via kolu shortcuts/prefs) reference kolu’s own UI. They stay in kolu, depending on the engine — never the reverse.

The interface that keeps the line clean: the engine exposes capture({ chrome, pace, size }, drive) where drive(page) is a kolu-domain closure. A recording’s kolu-specific knobs (hideRightPanel, collapseDock) are applied inside drive via kolu shortcuts — they are recording properties, never engine options. So the engine stays agnostic while each recording still carries its declarative display properties.

Folder layout (isolate the concept, per your call):

  • packages/screencast/ — the agnostic engine + its own nix (ffmpeg-full, xvfb, playwright) + .ts runner. Zero kolu imports; arrow points out. (Until it earns a package, it can live as a self-contained screencast/ module beside the e2e harness with the same no-kolu-imports rule — extraction stays a move, not a rewrite.)
  • packages/tests/recordings/<name>.recording.ts — one file per recording (kolu domain): { name, engine: {chrome, pace, size}, display: {hideRightPanel, collapseDock}, drive(world) }.
  • website/public/demo/<name>.{mp4,webm,webp} — committed outputs.
  • A just record <name> / record-all recipe drives a recording through the engine.

Follow-up: add the engine to the electricity tracker

When the engine folder lands, add a electricity.mdx row — @kolu/web-screencast publish? — alongside createSharedRoot and solid-xterm as a graduation candidate (agnostic + hard volatility; ③ pending a real second consumer).

Log — build journal

A running record of this work’s research, dead-ends, and decisions — raw material for a future blog post. Newest at the bottom.

The sections above predate the pivot

The plan above (sections 1–5) captures the original design — reuse recordVideo, fork at transcode. The log below is where it actually went: through a hard fps-vs-resolution wall, a second research pass, and out the other side to an OS-level x11grab capture in Chrome app-mode, then a Recording subsystem. The body will be revised to match once the subsystem lands.

1 · Plan + prior-art sweep. workflow #1 Picked up the kolu.dev/welcome thread from welcome-revamp. A fan-out research workflow (terminal recorders vs browser capture · embed patterns · CI determinism), adversarially verified, concluded: PTY recorders (vhs, asciinema) can’t capture kolu’s xterm-on-canvas pixels — it’s a raster GUI, not a replayable ANSI stream — so pixel capture via the existing Cucumber + Playwright evidence harness is the path. Embed pattern: poster-first autoplay/muted/loop/playsinline, mp4 + webm, reduced-motion.

2 · The crispness constraint. “Video needs to be crisp — no low-quality nonsense.” This became the spine. The evidence path records Playwright recordVideo = VP8 @ ~1 Mbit/s, 720p — fine for PR proof, too soft for a marketing page.

3 · Spike A (VP8). Reused the harness (KOLU_EVIDENCE) on a real worktree-agent scenario → 1280×720 VP8 → ffmpeg H.264. Smooth, but the text shimmered. Verdict: “could be crispier.”

4 · The fps-vs-resolution wall (the interesting part — good blog material). Chasing 2×, every in-Chrome capture API hit a structural limit:

  • CDP Page.startScreencast ignores deviceScaleFactor → caps at CSS pixels (1280); can’t do true 2×.
  • CDP Page.captureScreenshot can do 2× (via clip.scale), but each 2560×1440 grab is compositor-bound at ~260 ms → ~3 fps slideshow.
  • page.screenshot() gives true 2× but serialises behind the driver’s actions → ~10 frames over a 4 s scenario.
  • JPEG q92 instead of PNG sped the encode, but the scenario is only ~4 s → still ~13 frames.
  • Bonus bug: the capture loop started before the app loaded, so frame 0 was a blank white flash that strobed on every loop.

The lesson: real-time frame capture is either smooth-but-low-res/lossy or high-res-but-too-slow-to-be-smooth. You can’t win it from inside Chrome.

5 · Reset + second research pass. workflow #2 Reverted B to the smooth (supersampled-1280) screencast and ran a second fan-out workflow on existing solutions (virtual-time beginFrame · OS-level capture · purpose-built recorders · native Playwright options), adversarially verified. Verdict: move the capture boundary outside Chrome — Xvfb + ffmpeg -f x11grab of a headful 2× browser samples the framebuffer on its own fixed clock in physical pixels, so it’s structurally smooth and crisp at once. Also surfaced: no top dev tool ships an auto-captured hero clip — they’re “designed, not recorded” (Screen Studio etc.). We’re deliberately taking the reproducible-over-cinematic path.

6 · x11grab, working. Implemented KOLU_X11CAP: Xvfb at 2560×1440, headful Chrome at --force-device-scale-factor=2, ffmpeg -f x11grab at 30 fps, SIGINT to flush the moov atom. Gotcha that cost a cycle: nixpkgs ffmpeg is built --disable-xlib --disable-libxcb (no x11grab device — “Unrecognized option ‘draw_mouse’”) → switched the e2e devShell to ffmpeg-full. Result: 2560×1440, 30 fps, smooth + crisp. Verdict: “B looks good.”

7 · The browser-chrome realisation. x11grab records the whole window — the clip showed Chrome’s tab strip + localhost:38425 address bar. Reads as “a browser tab,” not “the app.” Fix: Chrome app-mode (--app=<url>) = the chromeless window an installed PWA uses. Driven via launchPersistentContext; the steps’ own page.goto("/") re-navigation re-applies the harness init scripts.

8 · Scope crystallised into a subsystem. Decided: drop A, one clip per slot, human pace, and N recordings — each its own file with declarative properties (hide right panel, collapse dock, app/browser chrome, pace): a lowy/hickey-clean Recording abstraction (data + script, separate from the capture pipeline). First two: ① PWA install/setup, ② installed-PWA demo (new terminal → git clone → cd → claude). Confirmed the pieces exist: kolu’s in-page install card is automatable (packages/client/src/WelcomeMoments.tsx, data-testid="welcome-install" / welcome-install-manual), and the terminal / git / palette steps for ② already exist in the step library — no new step code.

9 · Subsystem shipped. #1213 The agnostic engine (packages/tests/screencast/engine.ts, nix deps in screencast/shell.nix — top-level flake devShells untouched), the Recording modules (packages/tests/screencast/recordings), a When I record "<name>" dispatcher, and a just record recipe. Both clips captured: ② app-mode (chromeless), git clone kolu into ~/demo → cd → ls → claude launches with its trust prompt (15.6 s, 2560×1440); ① browser chrome, the palette Tutorial command summons the welcome with the Pin it card + expanded install steps (8.6 s). Captured locally, not on pu: kolu-ci-1 works (egress OK) but has no authenticated claude, so the demo’s climax only renders on a machine already running Claude Code — a deliberate, documented tradeoff (quality > box-purity; reproducibility lives in the recipe + modules, which do run on pu).

10 · Refined to one hero clip. Dropped the pwa-install recording — an in-clip browser→app transition isn’t reachable (bare Xvfb has no window manager, so F11 / the Fullscreen API can’t drop Chrome’s chrome; it would need two concatenated segments), and the app-mode demo already shows the chromeless app. The demo became the real loop: clone kolu → launch claude --dangerously-skip- permissions --model sonnet → accept the trust gate → ask “Explain this project, in 5 lines” → wait for the dock bucket to flip working → awaiting (the high-level state is data-bucket, not the raw data-agent-state) → glow the awaiting dock row and hold so the status change is unmissable. Two harness fixes made the dock track the live agent at all: (a) under KOLU_X11CAP, omit the KOLU_CLAUDE_*_DIR overrides so kolu watches the real ~/.claude/projects (the mock-harness temp dirs hid the launched claude); (b) a 240s timeout on the I record step (a real agent query exceeds the default budget). Result: a tight ~27s / ~1.3 MB clip. Reusable bits factored into helpers.ts (setupSingleTerminal, launchAgentAndAsk, theme-aware newTerminal) for the next single-terminal demo.
